reviews.review_msg: Dr. Wright's lectures are very engaging, and it's evident he cares profoundly about what he teaches. He is very approachable, and I've never had an issue asking him questions. However, the TAs are hard markers. MAKE SURE to attend their office hours if you are not happy with a grade, as we were not given a clear grading rubric for any assignments.

I have never encountered a prof who so clearly cares about his students. I asked Dr. Wright one question via email- he then invited me to speak with him after class, he gave me rich advice and spent nearly an hour just talking to me. His lectures cover interesting topics in a very straightforward, easy to understand, way. thank you, Dr. Wright

Dr. Wright knows what he's talking about and his lectures were really informative. His expectations for his evaluations were clear and he genuinely cares about how you are doing and wants to help. As the other reviews say, he does tend to ramble, but I still found him engaging.

He is a nice guy but he rambles about his own political opinion on different matters too much, sometimes, it seems like he's reaching to make a point. The lectures are long but overall enjoyable as he is interesting to listen to. The midterm is written, final is multiple choice. The group project is atrocious if you get into a bad group.

Steve is amazing! The lectures can be long as he tends to ramble, but the content was easy to understand. Make sure that you keep up with the readings as they are quite lengthy. His midterm consisted of short answers only but it is doable and he gives you at least 10 days to prepare as you only need to answer some of them during the exam.

Decent class, nice prof, just needs to have better time management (prolonged lectures). Biggest problem with this class was its poor inter-rater reliability. The two TAs took turns grading your different assignments but one of them marked CONSISTENTLY harder than the other which led to pretty big discrepancies between grades. Very frustrating.

Had really high expectations for this course as I heard good things about it but was kind of disappointed. The prof is really nice and passionate about the subject, but lectures felt really drawn out and unclear. Big discrepancy in the marking between the 2 TA's -- a lot of students noticed this. Super average experience with a lot of assignments.

Nice guy, cares a lot about the subject, but bad with time management. We almost never finished the lecture slides and were constantly running behind. Gave one 10-minute break during 3-hour lectures -- hard to keep energy and attention up. Group project spanned entire semester but wasn't that work-heavy. Good class, but frustrating.

nice prof. very interesting studies. midterm/2nd exam  based more on remote memorization rather than logic. test is very easy if you just memorize. I found assignments easy if you just follow the instructions.

I didn't mind this class. It was pretty interesting at times, and other times it would pretty much put me to sleep. Lectures are recorded, slides are posted online - very tempting to skip! Assignments and exams were fair, I thought. Not that hard of a course.

Professor Lau speaks much too fast and is difficult to understand because she moves at an extremely fast pace. It is unnecessary to attend lectures because she reads from her powerpoint slides. That being said, if you read the textbook before coming to class, the lecture is regurgitated. A very unegaging prof who does not know how to lecture.

Maitland is just an amazing person let alone professor. He constantly offers help, has clear guidelines for assignments and his exams were super clear! I was more focused on what his lectures talked about and I actually learned so much because he was so passionate and explains concepts well!! One of the best classes I have taken!!
Maitland is an amazing prof! He truly cares about his students, and all the marking criteria is straightforward! Would recommend!!

Maitland was a great prof! 3 hour lectures but he made them bearable. In-class activities for marks but basically just participation. He really encourages feedback, optional survey after every class for students to anonymously say what was unclear in the lecture. He is super clear about grading and truly wants his students to do well.

This was the first time Rob ever taught a class and he did a great job. He's passionate about the subject, encourages questions and is really down to earth. His leactures are highly entertaining AND informative. His exams are fair as are his assignments. The reading load is reasonable. I would take another class with him in a heartbeat.

Such a great guy. He really seems to love what he teaches, and he cares about his students' experiences in his class. I found him funny, and the classroom atmosphere he creates is really open and inviting (I voluntarily spoke in class and felt comfortable doing so, which is fairly rare for me)
Great prof. However im not a fan of her TA's.

Seems very enthusiastic during lecture but not as approachable in person. Lectures slides were organised. Daily participation tasks don't enrich learning and wastes paper. Forced us to sit in front half, which sucks for small bladdered people and those who think the projector slide view is better in the back rather up close.  TA very unhelpful. .

I had her for Psyc 363 at SFU. She's very clear and thorough. She uses lots of real world examples in her lectures which helped me a lot since I was new to some of the concepts. Overall, an excellent prof.

Scott is one of the most accessible and reasonable prof I have encountered. He tried out this thing for our covid semester called one week extension with no excuse needed. It fixed my studying habits with procrastination. There are many nice things I can say about him, but he is both a researcher and teacher. He sometimes teaches by not teaching.

I LOVE SCOTT! Just had for the fall 2020 semester and he has an absolute beauty! It was my first semester at SFU and he made it so easy to learn. Class discussions and lectures are very engaging! Scott wants you to do well and is always willing to make time for you. All I can say is I wish I could've taken this class in person. All the best Scott:)

One of my favourite professors ever! Very caring & really wants us to succeed. He's very open to suggestions on improving his class & responds to emails quickly. He gives lots of assignments so even if you do poorly on one, you can try to improve your grade with the next assignment. Really genuine & passionate about social psyc. Take his class!
One of the coolest professors at SFU. Psyc 363 with him is probably my favourite course I've taken during my undergrad so far.
Awesome class covering interesting and relevant topics! Scott clearly cares about his students and works hard to make the course engaging while keeping it clear and comprehensible.

Really enjoyed the class, lectures where interesting as well funny! Assignments where very clear as well interesting. He is easy to talk to as well will reply to emails quickly. Participation matters and exams are usually lecture heavy so best to go to class! Lectures are also recorded which helps with his slides
Interesting class and good first time instructor. a 3 hour lecture is tough but Scott tried to make it relevant to issues going on today which was eye opening.

I really enjoyed this class and think Scott was an engaging lecturer. He was always prepared, tries to make it interesting and clearly cares about the topic. You need to work for your grade, participate and take good notes since he doesn't spoon feed everything to you.
